title: Denaturing formaldehyde agarose gel-electrophoresis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bt0ri","text":"Protocol for separating total RNA using denaturing formaldehyde agarose gel electrophoresis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"54j4m","text":"This method can be used to separate larger RNAs in a range of 400-6000 nt, either for quality control or downstream Northern Blot Analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2u1g9","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dbgva","text":"Always wear gloves when handling hazardous materials.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2cl5q","text":"Always handle RNA cold and in an RNase free manner.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4u0ll","text":"Wear gloves when handling formaldehyde and ethidium bromide.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2t8e9","text":"Keep formaldehyde and gel under fume hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 10x MEN buffer: 200 mM MOPS, 50 mM NaOAc, 10 mM EDTA, pH = 7.0 Formaldehyde Ultra pure water Agarose

Recipe for one 100 mL gel (e.g. PeqLab Mini L electrophoresis chamber): Assemble gel chamber with suitable comb. Heat 1.3 g in 85 mL ultra pure water. Let cool. Under the hood, quickly add 10 mL 10x MEN buffer and 5 mL formaldehyde and pour gel.

For Northern Blot Analysis, 1-3 µg of RNA should be loaded per well. Adjust concentration and volume of all RNA samples by adding RNase-free ddH 2 O. Add equal amounts of 2x RNA Loading Dye. Denature RNA at 95˚C for 5 min. Snap cool on ice.
Add 1x MEN to the chamber (running buffer). Make sure wells are covered in buffer. Load RNA. Separate RNA at 5-6 V/cm for at least 30 minutes or longer, if desi
Document the gel using the BioRad ChemiDoc MP Imaging System. Use either the GelRed or the Ethidium bromide protocol.
creator: Anna Behle
